user.bio: my market is good

comment.body:
+ addition, what I always scrutinize in times like this, is that can market fall or not. Markets never fall when they are hedged. as said: if the odds are too good to be true, if the game seems to easy, then it is not. 
I see the moves on the skew. I also see the vol speculations ahead of the year.
And to me this is a discrepancy. 
These two weeks, every bottom tick that was yelled as "bearish!!" I said, no, buy the dip, upside bias. And I was right.
But I cannot be blinded. 
I scrutinize now the odds, scrutinize the momentum in hte positioning changes. And thinking about, what is the story... it's all just too obvious... what is the goal... where all of this wanna go

comment.body: It’s interesting that the Federal Funds Rate usually tanks with the market when it gets cut. Only time it diverges (cuts while the market rises) is during a divergence before a major correction. We’ve already had one cut. It’s actually been a leading indicator over the past 30 years.
comment.raw_body: It’s interesting that the Federal Funds Rate usually tanks with the market when it gets cut. Only time it diverges (cuts while the market rises) is during a divergence before a major correction. We’ve already had one cut. It’s actually been a leading indicator over the past 30 years.
